craft_guidance: 1. IDENTIFY ID SHAPE: numeric (auto-increment), UUIDv4 (random), UUIDv1 (timestamp+MAC monotonic), Snowflake (timestamp+worker), base32/base62 (often sequential). Sequential or time-based = walkable.
2. WALK THE SPACE: numeric IDs +1/-1 first; if 200+content delta, IDOR confirmed (Rule 6 — authz, NOT credential brute force). Cap PoC to 3-5 IDs.
3. MULTIPLE ENDPOINTS PER ID: /api/users/N, /api/users/N/orders, /api/orders?user_id=N. Object-level (BOLA) vs function-level (BFLA) are distinct findings.
4. NEGATIVE TESTS: ID = 0, -1, MAX_INT, '0' string, null. Edge handlers leak debug info.
5. INDIRECT IDOR: change non-obvious params (notification_id implies user_id; order_id implies account_id).
6. CROSS-APP REUSE: same ID space across multiple apps in ecosystem (main-app and admin-app share user_id) — test IDs from one app on the other.
7. MASS-ASSIGN OWNERSHIP: PATCH/PUT with `owner_id`/`user_id` → other user. If accepted, attacker takes over the resource (different bug class).
8. ID FORMAT TRANSFORMS: /users/123 vs /users/00123 vs /users/0x7B vs /users/123.json. Some routers normalize differently from auth middleware.
9. UUIDv1 EXPLOIT: UUIDv1 leaks creation time + MAC. Generate adjacent UUIDs by tweaking timestamp.
10. DO NOT EXFIL REAL DATA (Rule 7): demonstrate with 2-3 IDs returning distinct content. Don't dump the table.

numeric_id.description: IDOR probes for numeric integer identifiers — tests whether changing IDs returns other users data. NOTE: length_diff alone is indicative, not conclusive — paginated content, dynamic ads, or personalized widgets can cause length variance. Always verify semantically (different user data in response) before confirming.

probes.description: Replace baseline numeric ID with 2 — length diff with same status indicates a different record was returned
Replace baseline ID with 0 — may return admin or default account data; requires matching 200 status
Replace baseline ID with -1 — negative ID may bypass authorization checks; requires matching 200 status

uuid_id.description: IDOR probes for UUID-format identifiers — tests whether known or guessable UUIDs access other users data. Requires status match to avoid false positives from error pages.

probes.description: Replace UUID with a low-entropy sequential UUID — may hit admin or seed data records; requires 200 status match
Nil UUID injection — may bypass UUID validation or access a default resource; requires 200 status match

sequential_enum.description: Sequential ID enumeration IDOR — walks IDs above and below baseline to detect access to other records. Requires status match to filter error pages.

probes.description: Jump to ID 100 from baseline 1 — length diff with same 200 status and no error indicates a different user record is returned
ID zero probe — may return admin, system, or seed record; requires 200 status and length diff without access error

param_pollution.description: IDOR via HTTP Parameter Pollution — duplicate parameters may cause backend to use a different ID than the validator checked

probes.description: Appended duplicate id param — backend may use last value (2) while auth middleware used first value; requires 200 status
Reversed param order duplicate — some frameworks use first occurrence; sends attacker ID first; requires 200 status

method_override.description: IDOR via HTTP method change — endpoint may enforce auth on GET but not on PUT/PATCH/DELETE

probes.description: Re-send same request with PUT method — status 200 without 'method not allowed' may indicate missing auth on write path
Re-send same request with DELETE method — 200 without access error indicates missing authorization on delete path
Re-send with PATCH method — partial update succeeding without auth check confirms IDOR on write operations

composite_key.description: Multi-field IDOR -- swap one field of a composite key (org_id + user_id, tenant + resource) while keeping the other to access cross-tenant data

probes.description: Swap org_id/tenant_id to 1 while keeping own user_id -- tests if authorization checks both fields of composite key
Keep org_id but swap user_id to another user in different org -- tests cross-tenant access via partial key manipulation

encoded_id.description: IDOR via encoded/obfuscated identifiers -- base64-encoded IDs, hashids, JWT-embedded IDs, or encrypted IDs with discoverable patterns

probes.description: Base64-encoded ID 2 (Mg==) -- replace baseline base64 ID to test if server only checks format, not authorization
Base64-encoded ID 1 (MQ==) -- likely admin or first user record; test alongside own encoded ID
Raw numeric in encoded-ID field -- test if server accepts unencoded values, bypassing obfuscation layer entirely

bulk_operations.description: IDOR in bulk/batch endpoints -- delete-all, export, batch update may lack per-item authorization checks

probes.description: Inject other users resource IDs into bulk operation array -- tests if server validates ownership of each item in batch
Wildcard or 'all' in bulk selector -- test if server returns/affects all records without ownership check
